narration: Bubble sort works by comparing adjacent items in a list. If the left item is greater than the right item, they swap places.
The algorithm repeats this comparison and swap process from the beginning of the list until the list is sorted.
After each pass through the list, the largest unsorted element moves to its correct position at the end, reducing the number of comparisons needed in subsequent passes.
Define a function named bubbleSort that accepts a parameter called myList.
Create an outer for loop using a variable i that ranges from 0 to the length of myList minus 1.
Create an inner for loop using a variable j that ranges from 0 to the length of myList minus 1 minus i.
